annotations.label: Great circle puzzle setup

anchor.snippet: Suppose that a random variable has a uniform distribution on a unit sphere

annotations.note: Mathlib has `Measure.toSphere` giving a measure on the unit sphere, but no specific setup of the Borel-Kolmogorov great-circle puzzle.

annotations.label: Conditional density on the equator

anchor.snippet: If the coordinates are chosen so that the great circle is an equator

annotations.note: No specific computation of a uniform conditional density on the equator of a sphere appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional density on a line of longitude

anchor.snippet: If the great circle is a line of longitude

annotations.note: No formalization of the conditional density along a line of longitude on a sphere.

annotations.label: Conditional probability via limit of rings

anchor.snippet: Measure theory provides a way to define a conditional probability, using the limit of events

annotations.note: General conditional distribution via disintegration is in `condDistrib`, but the specific limit-of-rings construction on the sphere is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conditional probability via limit of lunes

anchor.snippet: is defined using a limit of the events

annotations.note: Same general disintegration tool exists, but no limit-of-lunes construction is formalized.

annotations.label: Random variables Φ and Λ on the sphere

anchor.snippet: denote two random variables taking values in

annotations.note: Mathlib has random variables generically but no specific definition of spherical-coordinate random variables Φ, Λ.

annotations.label: Volume element from coordinate transform

anchor.snippet: for which we obtain the volume element

annotations.note: No spherical-coordinate volume element `sin φ dφ dλ` is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Volume elements with one coordinate fixed

anchor.snippet: if either φ or λ is fixed, we get the volume elements

annotations.note: Conditional volume elements on great circles / longitudes are not formalized.

annotations.label: Uniform density yields different reference measures

anchor.snippet: has a uniform density with respect to

annotations.note: The observation that conditional uniform densities differ by reference-measure choice is not formalized as a Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Uniformly distributed random vector on the unit sphere

anchor.snippet: Consider a random vector

annotations.note: `Measure.toSphere` produces a natural rotation-invariant measure on the unit sphere, generalizing the uniform random vector, but no `IsUniform`-style declaration on the sphere is present.

annotations.label: Spherical polar parametrization

anchor.snippet: We begin by parametrizing the sphere with the usual spherical polar coordinates

annotations.note: Only 2D `polarCoord` exists in Mathlib; spherical polar coordinates on the 2-sphere are not formalized.

annotations.label: Random variables via arctan2

anchor.snippet: We can define random variables

annotations.note: Mathlib has no `arctan2` function and no construction of Φ, Λ random variables from a sphere vector.

annotations.label: Surface of a spherical cap wedge

anchor.snippet: Using the formulas for the surface area spherical cap and the spherical wedge

annotations.note: Surface-area formulas for spherical caps and wedges are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Joint cumulative distribution function

anchor.snippet: the probability is proportional to the surface area, giving the joint cumulative distribution function

annotations.note: The specific joint CDF of (Φ, Λ) derived from spherical area is not formalized.

annotations.label: Joint probability density function

anchor.snippet: The joint probability density function is then given by

annotations.note: The corresponding joint PDF `(1/4π) sin φ` for spherical coordinates is not formalized.

annotations.label: Independence of the coordinate random variables

anchor.snippet: are independent random variables

annotations.note: Mathlib has `IndepFun` generally, but no specific lemma showing Φ and Λ are independent for the spherical parametrization.

annotations.label: Conditional probability on the first octant

anchor.snippet: only the probability that the random vector lies in the first octant

annotations.note: No first-octant conditional probability computation on the sphere is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Conditional probability via limit using independence

anchor.snippet: We attempt to evaluate the conditional probability as a limit of conditioning on the events

annotations.note: The argument computing a conditional probability as a limit using independence of Φ, Λ is not formalized.

annotations.label: Alternate parametrization rotated by 90 degrees

anchor.snippet: Now we repeat the process with a different parametrization of the sphere

annotations.note: No rotated spherical parametrization construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Rotation preserves the joint density

anchor.snippet: Rotation is measure preserving so the density

annotations.note: Rotation invariance follows from Haar/`toSphere` infrastructure, but no specific lemma about invariance of this joint density is formalized.

annotations.label: Contradictory conditional probability via L'Hôpital

anchor.snippet: Using L'Hôpital's rule and differentiation under the integral sign

annotations.note: L'Hôpital's rule is in Mathlib, but the specific contradictory limit computation in the Borel-Kolmogorov proof is not formalized.

annotations.label: Conditional density not from event of probability zero

anchor.snippet: This shows that the conditional density cannot be treated as conditioning on an event of probability zero

annotations.note: The qualitative conclusion of the Borel-Kolmogorov paradox itself is not formalized in Mathlib.
